uncovered_criteria.ears: WHEN user confirms regeneration, THE SYSTEM SHALL trigger a fresh codebase scan and update L0/L1/L3
uncovered_criteria.analysis: No module declares ownership of this acceptance criterion. MOD-001 checkStaleness detects staleness (AC-1) but no module specifies the response path when user confirms regeneration. The natural implementation would involve: MOD-004 or MOD-006 orchestrating the user prompt at discover entry, MOD-003 scanCodebase for the fresh scan, and MOD-001 writeLayer to update L0/L1/L3. But no module's interface or acceptance_criteria_refs includes REQ-015-AC-2.
uncovered_criteria.suggested_fix: Add a 'handleStalenessResponse' interface to MOD-004 (mode-detector) or MOD-006 (discover-feature-mode) that: (1) presents the staleness warning from MOD-001.checkStaleness, (2) on user confirm, calls MOD-003.scanCodebase then MOD-001.writeLayer for L0/L1/L3, (3) on user decline, calls the risk recording per AC-3. Add REQ-015-AC-2 to the new interface's acceptance_criteria_refs.

uncovered_criteria.ears: WHEN user chooses to proceed despite warning, THE SYSTEM SHALL record the acknowledged staleness as a known risk
uncovered_criteria.analysis: No module declares ownership of this acceptance criterion. No interface specifies where or how 'acknowledged staleness' is recorded. Is it written to the feature's discover.json? To a risk section in profile? To ModeContext? The spec is silent on all of these questions.
uncovered_criteria.suggested_fix: Define the storage location for acknowledged staleness (e.g., a 'known_risks' field in the feature discover artifact or a 'staleness_acknowledged' flag in ModeContext). Add a corresponding interface or extend an existing one (e.g., MOD-004 resolveFlowMode output could include 'stalenessAcknowledged: boolean'). Add REQ-015-AC-3 to the interface's acceptance_criteria_refs.

findings.description: REQ-015 is only partially covered. MOD-001 checkStaleness handles detection (AC-1) but the user interaction path (AC-2: regeneration, AC-3: acknowledged risk) has no module ownership. An executor would implement staleness detection but have no spec guidance for the response flow.
findings.evidence: grep for 'REQ-015-AC-2' and 'REQ-015-AC-3' across all 8 spec module files returns zero matches. MOD-001 acceptance_criteria_refs for checkStaleness only includes REQ-015-AC-1.
findings.realist_check: The staleness warning itself (AC-1) works. The gap is in response handling — users would see a warning but the system would not know how to act on their choice. This is a spec gap that causes rework during build, not a runtime failure. Severity MAJOR is appropriate.

INV-001.enforcement: MOD-004 auto-resolves greenfield. MOD-006/007/008 apply feature branches additively. MOD-008 adds profile write as sole greenfield change.

INV-002.enforcement: MOD-001 writeLayer returns L2_DISABLED error. MOD-001 readConfig provides flag. MOD-002 skips L2 extraction. MOD-005 falls back to structural-only conflict detection.

INV-003.enforcement: MOD-006 featureArtifactOutput references profile by path. MOD-005 reads profile via MOD-001 API, not by copying.

INV-004.enforcement: MOD-002 nfr_constraints explicitly states 'Writer must never modify specs/ artifacts'. MOD-001 writeLayer targets .nopilot/profile/ only.

INV-005.enforcement: MOD-003 scanCodebase accepts scanThresholdFiles config. Output includes parallelized boolean indicating sub-agent split.

ac_coverage.REQ-001-AC-4: MOD-001 interface design (no trigger timing in API)
ac_coverage.REQ-001-AC-5: MOD-001 owned schema files + readLayer validation

ac_coverage.REQ-002-AC-2: MOD-002 mode input enum, MOD-008 nfr (fires for both modes)

ac_coverage.REQ-003-AC-2: MOD-001 readLayer returns null on missing file

ac_coverage.REQ-008-AC-2: MOD-002 mergeDomainModel (conflicts output), MOD-005 detectConflicts (domain_model type)

ac_coverage.REQ-015-AC-2: MOD-004 handleStalenessResponse (regenerate path → MOD-003 scanCodebase + MOD-001 writeLayer)
ac_coverage.REQ-015-AC-3: MOD-004 handleStalenessResponse (proceed path → ModeContext.stalenessAcknowledged → feature discover artifact known_risks)

verdict_justification: Original review found 1 MAJOR gap (REQ-015-AC-2/AC-3). Fix applied: MOD-004 gained handleStalenessResponse interface covering regeneration trigger and acknowledged staleness recording. All 52 acceptance criteria now have module ownership. 15/15 requirements complete. 5/5 invariants enforced. Zero undeclared behaviors. Zero critical findings.

one_flow_conditional_branches.evidence: MOD-006/007/008 are all 'modifications, not replacement' to existing discover.md/spec.md/build.md. No new commands introduced.

workflow_output_is_memory_source.evidence: MOD-002 extractors read exclusively from existing NoPilot artifacts. No new data generation.

intent_and_structure_separated.evidence: L2 physically separated, independently disableable via config.l2_enabled. MOD-005 degrades to structural-only when L2 absent.

design_for_removal.evidence: Each module has bounded dependency edges. L2 independently toggleable. JSON-per-layer storage means any layer deletable without cascading.

context_must_not_overgeneralize.evidence: MOD-004 two-dimensional detection. MOD-003 produces partial types that supplement, not replace. First-time onboarding scans then asks user.

constraint_compliance.constraint: No new runtime or external service dependencies
constraint_compliance.detail: Ajv listed as library dependency for JSON Schema validation. Consistent with discover tech_direction.
All profile and config files under .nopilot/.
All modules use src/profile/*.ts or commands/*.md modifications.
INV-001 enforced. Sole addition is profile write after build.

global_coherence_check.notes: Spec faithfully decomposes discover's 14 MVP features into 8 modules (5 infrastructure + 3 command modifications). No scope creep. Drift score 8/100 (negligible). REQ-015 gap resolved by adding handleStalenessResponse to MOD-004.
